subject: Re: Local refinement of particles from a 2D lattice in M (MCore)
body:
Hi Juha, hi Alister,

I can't help but remember when I was processing patches of 2D crystal data
using FREALIGN. I implemented restraints (priors) in the loss function to
prevent the orientations from drifting too far away during the refinement,
exactly for the reasons described. FREALIGN used Powell's method so I
believe it's a fairly similar case. Maybe priors could be implemented into
MCore, if this really turns out to be an issue.

Cheers,

--
Ricardo Diogo Righetto


Em seg., 16 de dez. de 2024 às 19:59, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
escreveu:

> Hi Juha,
>
> Great question! My understanding is that pose parameters are refined in
> the same way as other model parameters, they are optimized directly with
> the L-BFGS optimiser.
> This means that they are not explicitly constrained, however they should
> be refined only very locally and change by small amounts at each iteration.
> When I was working on similar data I never had issues with particles
> jumping around the lattice in M, although the spacing in my lattice was
> relatively large at 12nm.
>
> If you visualize your positions/orientations and notice this is causing
> issues please report back!
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On Mon, 16 Dec 2024 at 00:22, Juha Huiskonen <juha.huiskonen@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> When particle poses are refined in MCore, how are the search parameters
>> (shifts, rotations) limited around the initial pose?
>>
>> My particles come from a 2D lattice, and I would like to make sure that
>> 1. M does not match the neighboring unit and 2. M does not search too far
>> from the initial pose (which I know from geometry of the lattice and have
>> limited as a prior in RELION). The structure I am currently averaging is a
>> patch from this lattice i.e. more than one repeating unit.
>>
>> Best wishes,
>> Juha
>>
>> --
